active_alerts.description: Please see station personnel or use a station call box to request assistance.

Skipped outbound stops:
Tremont St opp St Mary's High School
Market St @ Tremont St

To complete track improvement work, the Greenbush, Kingston, and Fall River/New Bedford Lines will originate and terminate at Braintree during the following weekends:
• April 18-19 and 25-26
• May 9-10

Passengers can use the Red Line for service between South Station and Braintree. Inbound Greenbush Line trains will terminate at Braintree after stopping at Weymouth Landing/East Braintree, a journey of approximately 9 minutes. Outbound Greenbush Line trains will originate at Braintree approximately 10 minutes prior to the schedule Weymouth Landing/East Braintree departure time.

Affected stations:
South Station
JFK/UMass
Quincy Center
Braintree

Express buses will serve all stops between Framingham and Wellesley Hills and then run express to either Back Bay or South Station.

Local buses will serve all stops (except Wellesley Farms and Lansdowne) between Wellesley Hills and either Back Bay or South Station.

There will be timed transfers between the local and express routes at Wellesley Hills.

Travel Times:
Riders using the shuttles should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to South Station from Framingham should budget approximately 20 extra minutes if taking the express shuttles and up to 70 extra minutes if taking the local shuttles.

Affected Stations and Shuttle Stop Locations:
South Station – South Station Bus Terminal Gates 3-6
Back Bay – Dartmouth St @ Back Bay Station (Pickup), Clarendon St @ Back Bay Busway (Dropoff)
Lansdowne – Not Served
Boston Landing – Guest St @ New Balance Wy
Newtonville – Washington St @ Walnut St
West Newton – Washington St @ Highland St
Auburndale – Auburn St @ Melrose St
Wellesley Farms – Not Served
Wellesley Hills – Washington St @ Abbott Rd
Wellesley Square – Central St @ Railroad Ave
Natick Center – South Ave @ Washington St
West Natick – W Central St @ West Natick Parking Lot
Framingham – Waverly St @ Framingham Station

This is to accommodate track improvement work and the replacement of 14,000 railroad ties and is scheduled to complete mid-May, 2026.

Single-track operations will be in effect throughout this period so boarding areas may change.

Temporarily Suspended Inbound Trains:
•	126 (8:49am from Newburyport)
•	1132 (10:53am from Beverly)
•	46 (1:47pm from Rockport)
•	1150 (3:23 from Beverly)

Temporarily Suspended Outbound Trains:
•	125 (8:35am to Newburyport)
•	1129 (9:35am to Beverly)
•	39 (12:05pm to Rockport)
•	11:47am (2:05pm to Beverly)

There are accessible mini-high platforms at the Western end of both the Track 1 and Track 2 sides. However, the existing pedestrian path between the two sides is long and includes steep slopes, especially on the Crest Road bridge over the tracks. Designs are underway to provide for a fully compliant pathway.

Central Subway:
D Branch service will operate between Union Sq and Government Center. E Branch service will operate between Medford/Tufts and Heath St.

Travel Times:
Riders using shuttles bus service should budget ample extra travel time. For example, a rider travelling to Park Street from Riverside should budget at least an additional 45 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute. Riders downtown are encouraged to use the Orange Line between North Station and Back Bay.

Affected stations:
Copley
Hynes Convention Center
Kenmore
Saint Mary's Street

• This is to allow signal improvement work.

• A diversion schedule will be in effect.

• Passengers can use the Orange Line for service between North Station and Oak Grove.

• There will be normal Commuter Rail weekend train service between Oak Grove and Haverhill.

• There will be no shuttle buses provided for this diversion.

• Riders using the Orange Line should budget extra travel time. For example, a rider traveling to North Station from Oak Grove should budget at least 10 minutes of travel in addition to their regular trip time.

Skipped outbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl

Skipped inbound stops:
136 Georgetowne Pl
Scheduled to complete sometime in 2027, the Reconstruction of Foster Street includes widening and resurfacing with the addition of bicycle lanes.

To travel from the North Station platform to the Fitchburg platform, travel east on Maple St towards Main St. At Main St, turn left and continue to Railroad St. At Railroad St, turn left and continue to the station, where a ramp to the Fitchburg platform is located next the small parking lot. To travel from the Fitchburg platform to the North Station platform, travel to the east end of the platform and proceed down the ramp to the small parking lot. Continue east on Railroad St to Main St. At Main St, turn right and continue to Maple St. At Maple St, turn right and continue to the walkway that leads to the platform.

Affected lines:
Foxboro Event Service
Providence/Stoughton Line

• Local and express shuttle buses will be available.

• This is to accommodate signal improvement work.

• A diversion schedule will be in effect.

• An express shuttle will operate nonstop between North Station and Swampscott.

• A local shuttle will operate between North Station and Swampscott, making all local stops.

• Riders using the shuttle buses should budget extra travel time. For example, a rider traveling to North Station from Swampscott should budget up to 40 minutes additional travel on the express shuttle and up to 70 minutes additional travel on the local shuttle.

Shuttle bus pick-up/drop-off locations:

North Station:
Nashua St @ Red Auerbach Way

Chelsea:
Inbound, Everett Ave @ Vale St (MBTA Route 112 bus stop). Outbound, Everett Ave @ Maple St (MBTA Route 112 bus stop).

Wonderland:
Wonderland Blue Line Station Busway (behind Berth E)

Lynn:
Lynn Station Busway Berth #1 (MBTA Routes 441 & 442 bus stop)

Swampscott:
Railroad Ave @ Swampscott Station

Shuttle buses will continue to replace B Branch service between Boston College and Kenmore through Thursday, April 30.

For example, a rider travelling to Park Street from Riverside should budget at least an additional 45 minutes of travel in additional to their regular commute. Riders downtown are encouraged to use the Orange Line between North Station and Back Bay.

• This is to accommodate signal improvement work.

• A diversion schedule will be in effect.

• Passengers can use the Red Line for travel between Porter and Park St.

• Passengers can use the Green Line for travel between Park St and North Station.

• Passengers traveling to/from North Station should budget extra travel time. For example, a rider traveling to North Station from Porter should budget at least 20 minutes of travel in addition to their regular trip time.

To exit the Red Line, use nearby South Station Elevator 918. To exit the Silver Line, take South Station Elevator 927 to the Alewife platform, then take South Station Elevator 919 to the Airport/Design Center/Chelsea platform. At the Silver Line platform, use South Station Elevator 918 to access the lobby. To board the Red Line, use South Station Elevator 918 located on the opposide side of the lobby.

Oxford St @ Market St

The escalator will remain out of service for the foreseeable future. This decision was made after a thorough inspection determined that corrosion on the escalator’s steel truss was so severe that it could not be repaired further and that a complete replacement of the escalator would be required. Replacing the escalator at this location requires a more extended unit meeting modern escalator safety requirements. This longer unit will require significant alteration to the station entrance to meet code requirements. The MBTA has currently begun design work to understand what station upgrades will be needed.

Due to these complexities, we expect the escalator will remain out of service for a significant time. We recognize the challenges this poses to our riders, and please know we are working to identify a solution as quickly as possible. In the meantime, Government Center Station, which is fully accessible and serves the Blue Line, is under a quarter-mile from Bowdoin Station.
